Apple___Apple_scab.immediate: Remove and destroy infected leaves and fruit from the tree and ground.
Apple___Apple_scab.chemical: Apply myclobutanil or captan fungicide at early spring through petal fall.
Apple___Apple_scab.organic: Use sulfur-based fungicide or Bordeaux mixture before infection period.
Apple___Apple_scab.prevention: Prune trees to improve air circulation, rake fallen leaves in autumn, plant resistant cultivars.

Apple___Black_rot.immediate: Prune out infected branches 6-8 inches below the visible canker.
Apple___Black_rot.chemical: Apply captan or thiophanate-methyl fungicide during growing season.
Apple___Black_rot.organic: Use copper-based fungicide, remove mummified fruit from trees.
Apple___Black_rot.prevention: Remove dead or diseased wood during winter pruning, avoid wounding trees.

Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.immediate: Remove galls from cedar trees within 2 miles if possible.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.chemical: Apply myclobutanil or mancozeb fungicide from pink through petal fall.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.organic: Use sulfur-based fungicide applied preventatively in spring.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust.prevention: Remove nearby red cedar and juniper trees, plant resistant apple varieties.

Apple___healthy.prevention: Continue good cultural practices: proper pruning, fertilization, and pest monitoring.

Blueberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper soil pH (4.5-5.5), provide adequate irrigation and mulching.

Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.immediate: Remove and destroy infected leaves and shoots.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.chemical: Apply sulfur-based or myclobutanil fungicide at first sign of disease.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.organic: Use neem oil or potassium bicarbonate spray weekly.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Prune for good air circulation, avoid overhead irrigation, plant resistant varieties.

Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy.prevention: Continue regular monitoring, proper pruning, and balanced fertilization.

Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.immediate: No effective removal; focus on prevention for next season.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.chemical: Apply azoxystrobin or pyraclostrobin fungicide at tasseling stage.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.organic: Use copper-based fungicide at early symptom stage.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray.prevention: Rotate with non-host crops (soybean), till crop residue, plant resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.immediate: No effective removal; monitor for spread to upper leaves.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.chemical: Apply azoxystrobin or mancozeb fungicide if infection reaches upper canopy before tasseling.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.organic: Use sulfur-based fungicide at early symptom appearance.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_.prevention: Plant resistant hybrids, avoid late planting, reduce irrigation frequency.

Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.immediate: No effective removal; monitor disease progression.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.chemical: Apply strobilurin fungicide (azoxystrobin) at early symptom stage.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.organic: Use copper-based fungicide as a preventative spray.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight.prevention: Rotate crops with non-host crops, till under crop residue, use resistant hybrids.

Corn_(maize)___healthy.prevention: Continue proper fertility management, timely planting, and weed control.

Grape___Black_rot.immediate: Remove and destroy mummified fruit and infected canes during pruning.
Grape___Black_rot.chemical: Apply mancozeb or myclobutanil fungicide at pre-bloom through post-bloom.
Grape___Black_rot.organic: Use copper-based fungicide or sulfur dust applied weekly.
Grape___Black_rot.prevention: Prune vines to improve air circulation, remove infected plant debris, use trellising.

Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).immediate: Remove and destroy infected arms and canes, re-train replacement wood.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).chemical: Apply trunk wound protectants containing sodium arsenite (where permitted).
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).organic: Prune only in dry weather, apply pruning wound sealants with boron.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles).prevention: Avoid large pruning cuts, maintain vine vigor, minimize trunk injury.

Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.immediate: Remove severely infected leaves from the vineyard floor.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.chemical: Apply mancozeb or copper-based fungicide during early season.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.organic: Use Bordeaux mixture or copper soap at leaf emergence.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo.prevention: Ensure good canopy management, proper trellising, and balanced nutrition.

Grape___healthy.prevention: Continue proper pruning, canopy management, and disease monitoring.

Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).immediate: Remove infected trees immediately to prevent spread to adjacent trees.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).chemical: No cure available; apply foliar nutrients and systemic insecticides for psyllid control.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).organic: Use kaolin clay sprays and neem oil for psyllid management.
Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening).prevention: Use certified disease-free nursery stock, control Asian citrus psyllid with reflective mulch.

Peach___Bacterial_spot.immediate: Remove severely infected twigs and branches during dormant season.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericide during dormant stage and at petal fall.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.organic: Use copper hydroxide or Bordeaux mixture in early spring.
Peach___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Plant resistant cultivars, avoid overhead irrigation, apply dormant copper spray.

Peach___healthy.prevention: Continue proper pruning, thinning, and pest monitoring practices.

Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.immediate: Remove infected leaves and plants promptly.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericide every 7-10 days during wet weather.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.organic: Use Bacillus subtilis-based biofungicide or copper soap.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Use disease-free seed, practice crop rotation, avoid working in wet fields.

Pepper,_bell___healthy.prevention: Continue proper irrigation, fertilization, and integrated pest management.

Potato___Early_blight.immediate: Remove infected foliage and destroy away from field.
Potato___Early_blight.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide every 7-10 days.
Potato___Early_blight.organic: Use compost tea or Bacillus subtilis-based products.
Potato___Early_blight.prevention: Rotate crops with non-solanaceous crops, use certified seed potatoes.

Potato___Late_blight.immediate: Remove and destroy all infected plant material immediately.
Potato___Late_blight.chemical: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil fungicide preventatively every 5-7 days.
Potato___Late_blight.organic: Use copper-based organic fungicides at first sign of disease.
Potato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, ensure good drainage, destroy volunteer potatoes.

Potato___healthy.prevention: Continue proper crop rotation, use certified seed, and monitor for pests.

Raspberry___healthy.prevention: Maintain proper pruning cycle, trellising, and adequate air flow.

Soybean___healthy.prevention: Continue proper crop rotation, soil management, and pest scouting.

Squash___Powdery_mildew.immediate: Remove heavily infected leaves and destroy them.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.chemical: Apply sulfur-based or myclobutanil fungicide at first sign of disease.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.organic: Use neem oil or potassium bicarbonate spray weekly.
Squash___Powdery_mildew.prevention: Provide good air circulation, avoid overhead watering, plant resistant varieties.

Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.immediate: Remove infected leaves and improve air circulation.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.chemical: Apply captan or thiram fungicide during growing season.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.organic: Use neem oil spray or Bacillus subtilis biofungicide.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch.prevention: Use disease-free transplants, practice crop rotation, renovate beds after harvest.

Strawberry___healthy.prevention: Continue proper irrigation, mulching, and annual bed renovation.

Tomato___Bacterial_spot.immediate: Remove affected leaves immediately to prevent spread.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.chemical: Apply copper-based bactericide (e.g., copper oxychloride) every 7-10 days.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.organic: Use neem oil solution (2%) or potassium bicarbonate spray.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot.prevention: Practice crop rotation, avoid overhead watering, ensure good air circulation.

Tomato___Early_blight.immediate: Remove infected leaves and destroy them away from the garden.
Tomato___Early_blight.chemical: Apply chlorothalonil or mancozeb fungicide every 7-10 days.
Tomato___Early_blight.organic: Use compost tea or Bacillus subtilis-based products.
Tomato___Early_blight.prevention: Mulch around plants, water at soil level, rotate crops annually.

Tomato___Late_blight.immediate: Remove and destroy all infected plant material immediately.
Tomato___Late_blight.chemical: Apply mancozeb or chlorothalonil fungicide preventatively.
Tomato___Late_blight.prevention: Plant resistant varieties, avoid overhead watering, ensure good spacing.

Tomato___Leaf_Mold.immediate: Remove infected leaves and improve air circulation.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.organic: Use neem oil or copper-based sprays.
Tomato___Leaf_Mold.prevention: Space plants properly, avoid overhead watering, use drip irrigation.

Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.immediate: Remove infected leaves and destroy them.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.organic: Use compost tea or neem oil.
Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot.prevention: Mulch around plants, water at soil level, rotate crops.

Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.immediate: Spray leaves with strong stream of water to dislodge mites.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.chemical: Apply miticides like abamectin or bifenthrin.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.organic: Use neem oil, insecticidal soap, or introduce predatory mites.
Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider.prevention: Maintain proper humidity, avoid drought stress, inspect plants regularly.

Tomato___Target_Spot.immediate: Remove infected leaves and destroy them.
Tomato___Target_Spot.prevention: Practice crop rotation, ensure good air circulation, avoid overhead watering.

Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.immediate: Remove infected plants immediately to prevent spread.
Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties, control whiteflies, use row covers.

Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.immediate: Remove infected plants and destroy them.
Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus.prevention: Use virus-resistant varieties, disinfect tools, control aphids.

Tomato___healthy.prevention: Continue good cultural practices: proper spacing, watering, and fertilization.
